schedule_note: Live dogfood board for building Switchboard with Switchboard. This project is intentionally isolated from Maxwell and Helm. It starts as an experiment: humans may still commit normally, but agents should use the working agreement, presence, claim_next, evidence-backed completion, and reconcile loop wherever possible.
executive_summary: Switchboard is the control plane for cross-LLM and cloud-agent collaboration: a small protocol and runtime that lets heterogeneous agents register presence, claim resources, receive interrupts, take scheduled work, report cost, and prove work through git-backed provenance. This board is the live lab for making the product coordinate its own development.
timeline_note: P0 focuses on proving the loop end to end before treating it as production infrastructure: isolated board namespace, protocol surface, adapters, enforcement tiers, claim_next dispatch, Tally, and reconciliation. The board should expose missing primitives quickly rather than hide them behind docs.

working_agreement.definition_of_done: merged to master with a recorded merge SHA; agents never self-set Done
working_agreement.main_writes: PR preferred; direct master commits require a reconcile note and recorded SHA
working_agreement.merge_strategy: squash or fast-forward; record the resulting master SHA
session_start_sequence: complete_claim with branch/head_sha/pr evidence when finished
working_agreement.agent_completion_rule: complete_claim moves work to In Review; only merge/reconcile authority sets Done

tasks.title: Add isolated Switchboard project namespace and seed live P0 board
tasks.description: Create a third physical project namespace for Switchboard so product-planner work can be tracked without polluting Maxwell or Helm. Seed a compact P0 board that mirrors the primitives being built: protocol, adapters, enforcement, dispatch, Tally, reconcile, and dogfood operations.
tasks.entry_criteria: Projectplanner already supports isolated project registries and seed files.
tasks.exit_criteria: store.PROJECTS exposes switchboard; startup seeds switchboard.db from seeds/switchboard_seed_plan.json; /api/projects and MCP can address project=switchboard; tests prove the seed loads.
tasks.deliverable: Switchboard project registry entry plus seed board.

tasks.title: Deploy and verify project=switchboard on plan.taikunai.com
tasks.description: After the registry lands, refresh the live planner service and MCP service, then verify the project switcher, board payload, MCP search_tasks, board_summary, get_working_agreement, claim_next, and reconcile all work against project=switchboard.
tasks.entry_criteria: DOGFOOD-1 is merged or otherwise present on the host.
tasks.exit_criteria: Live web and MCP endpoints show the Switchboard board and do not route Switchboard writes into Maxwell or Helm.

tasks.title: Run the first full dogfood agent session
tasks.description: Use the Switchboard board to coordinate a real Switchboard change: get_working_agreement, register_agent, drain inbox, claim_next, complete_claim with branch/SHA/PR evidence, then reconcile. Capture every mismatch between desired and actual agent behavior.
tasks.entry_criteria: Live switchboard board is reachable through MCP and has at least one ready task.
tasks.exit_criteria: One real change is coordinated through the loop and its evidence is visible on the task.

tasks.title: Write the operator runbook for Switchboard self-coordination
tasks.description: Document how humans and agents use the Switchboard project safely: when to claim, how to report evidence, when direct master commits are allowed, how reconcile findings get cleared, and how not to cross-write Maxwell or Helm.
tasks.entry_criteria: At least one dogfood session has exposed the actual rough edges.
tasks.exit_criteria: A runbook exists and is referenced by the working agreement.

tasks.title: Backfill provenance for pre-dogfood Switchboard work
tasks.description: Record the commits and evidence for Switchboard primitives that landed before the live dogfood board existed. This gives the new board a first ready task without pretending historical work was completed through the protocol.
tasks.entry_criteria: Switchboard seed board exists and earlier commits are visible in git history.
tasks.exit_criteria: Relevant pre-dogfood commits are noted on the right tasks and reconcile has a known baseline.

tasks.title: Lock IXP/TXP/OXP minimum conformance profile
tasks.description: Turn the PRD and specs into the smallest profile an adapter must satisfy: working agreement, presence, inbox, resource leases, claim_next, completion evidence, Tally reporting, and reconcile visibility.
tasks.entry_criteria: PRD, IXP, runtime adapter, claim_next, interrupt, and Tally specs exist in docs.
tasks.exit_criteria: A conformance matrix names required, optional, and experimental operations for P0 adapters.

tasks.title: Version protocol envelopes and compatibility policy
tasks.description: Add explicit protocol versioning, capability negotiation, and compatibility rules so older adapters can coexist with new server features without silent breakage.
tasks.entry_criteria: P0 conformance profile is written.
tasks.exit_criteria: Every handshake response includes protocol/profile identifiers and adapters know how to fail closed on incompatible versions.

tasks.title: Build conformance fixtures for protocol adapters
tasks.description: Create a reusable test fixture that can exercise register, inbox, claim resource, claim_next, complete_claim evidence, Tally report, and reconcile against a throwaway project database.
tasks.entry_criteria: Protocol versioning and profile requirements are stable enough to test.
tasks.exit_criteria: Adapters can run a single smoke command and get pass/fail output against the P0 profile.

tasks.title: Claude Code adapter pack
tasks.description: Package the session-start ritual for Claude Code: working agreement, register, inbox drain, claim_next, resource claims, completion evidence, and reconcile summary. The goal is to make correct protocol behavior automatic instead of prompt-dependent.
tasks.entry_criteria: P0 handshake and completion semantics are documented.
tasks.exit_criteria: Claude Code sessions can opt into a small adapter pack that performs the P0 handshake reliably.

tasks.title: Codex adapter pack
tasks.description: Create the Codex-side wrapper/instructions that automatically loads the working agreement, registers session fidelity, checks inbox, claims ready work, and reports evidence on completion.
tasks.entry_criteria: Switchboard board is live and Claude Code adapter shape is known.
tasks.exit_criteria: Codex can start a Switchboard task without a human manually reciting the protocol.
tasks.deliverable: Codex adapter instructions and smoke test.

tasks.title: Cursor and raw OpenAI loop adapters
tasks.description: Provide minimal adapter packs for Cursor and raw OpenAI agent loops so the protocol is not tied to one vendor runner.
tasks.entry_criteria: Codex adapter shows the expected wrapper shape.
tasks.exit_criteria: Cursor and raw OpenAI loops can pass the P0 conformance fixture.

tasks.title: LangGraph runtime adapter
tasks.description: Map Switchboard primitives into a LangGraph runner without confusing LangGraph's in-process graph semantics with cross-agent coordination.
tasks.entry_criteria: Version/profile negotiation exists and one simpler adapter is proven.
tasks.exit_criteria: A LangGraph workflow can register, claim, report progress, and respect interrupts through Switchboard.

tasks.title: Control fidelity registry and session advertisement
tasks.description: Every registered session should advertise the highest interrupt/control tier it can honor: advisory poll, hook-level deny, or runner kill. The board should make weak-control sessions visible instead of pretending all agents are equally steerable.
tasks.entry_criteria: register_agent stores a control/fidelity blob.
tasks.exit_criteria: Active-agent views and protocol responses expose control tier consistently.
tasks.deliverable: Control fidelity fields in runtime and docs.

tasks.title: Hook-level deny enforcement prototype
tasks.description: Implement a real deny point for runtimes that support pre-tool hooks: denied file/resource actions should be blocked before the risky tool call, with an inbox message and audit event.
tasks.entry_criteria: At least one adapter can call a pre-tool coordination hook.
tasks.exit_criteria: A conflicting file/resource action is denied in a real adapter smoke test and the task records the event.

tasks.title: Runner kill tier design and guardrails
tasks.description: Design the strongest interrupt tier for managed runners: what can be killed, who can kill it, how partial work is preserved, and what audit/event trail is required.
tasks.entry_criteria: Hook-level deny semantics are proven in at least one adapter.
tasks.exit_criteria: Runner kill policy and API shape are documented with safety limits.

tasks.title: Dependency-aware claim_next scheduler
tasks.description: Keep claim_next as the moment Switchboard becomes an active scheduler: it must hand agents ready work, respect dependencies and active claims, and avoid turning the board into a passive reporting surface.
tasks.entry_criteria: Tasks have depends_on and claim leases.
tasks.exit_criteria: claim_next returns only unblocked work and blocks dependents until merge-derived Done.

tasks.title: Priority, capability, and budget scoring for claim_next
tasks.description: Move beyond deterministic first-ready selection. Score candidate tasks by lane, capability match, risk, budget, blocking status, and current fleet load.
tasks.entry_criteria: Basic claim_next and rough task tally exist.
tasks.exit_criteria: claim_next explains why a task was selected and respects at least capability and budget constraints.

tasks.title: Human override and revoke-claim flow
tasks.description: Let a human operator revoke, reassign, or reprioritize claimed work without corrupting task state or losing evidence.
tasks.entry_criteria: Claim scoring and control fidelity are visible.
tasks.exit_criteria: Operators can revoke a claim, notify the agent, and preserve partial evidence.

tasks.title: Two-stream cost ledger projection
tasks.description: Implement the rough but useful Tally wedge early: gateway-measured spend and agent-reported spend should both attach to project/task/claim/agent and remain visibly separated by confidence.
tasks.entry_criteria: Activity log and claim IDs exist.
tasks.exit_criteria: report_usage and task_tally show cost by source and confidence for a claimed task.

tasks.title: Outcome and KPI mapping primitives
tasks.description: Add the missing commercial wedge: outcomes must link to KPIs so Switchboard can say what a task achieved and what it cost, not just that tokens were spent.
tasks.entry_criteria: Spend records attach cleanly to tasks and claims.
tasks.exit_criteria: A task can record an outcome, link it to a KPI, and show cost per KPI movement.

tasks.title: Cost-per-outcome board surface
tasks.description: Expose Tally where operators actually look: task detail, board rollups, and project-level KPI movement. Even rough economics should be visible early.
tasks.entry_criteria: Outcome/KPI mapping APIs exist.
tasks.exit_criteria: Operators can inspect cost per outcome without querying raw tables.

tasks.title: Working agreement and git provenance lifecycle
tasks.description: Make the board the source of truth for where work is: step-zero working agreement, In Review completion, PR-open evidence, merge-SHA Done transition, and local reconcile findings for drift.
tasks.entry_criteria: Tasks and GitHub webhook path exist.
tasks.exit_criteria: Agents cannot honestly claim Done without merge evidence; reconcile flags legacy or drifted task states.
tasks.deliverable: Working agreement, git_state, PR webhook lifecycle, reconcile.

tasks.title: GitHub reachability and content reconciliation
tasks.description: Extend local reconcile with external checks: branch/head reachability, PR state, merge commit presence on master, and optionally content proof that files changed as claimed.
tasks.entry_criteria: Local git_state table and GitHub webhook lifecycle exist.
tasks.exit_criteria: reconcile can distinguish local board contradictions from external GitHub drift.

tasks.title: Scheduled reconcile and alerting
tasks.description: Run reconcile automatically and notify humans/agents when the board lies, a claim is stale, or a task is In Review without branch/PR evidence.
tasks.entry_criteria: External reconcile checks exist.
tasks.exit_criteria: Drift findings are scheduled, surfaced, and actionable.
tasks.deliverable: Scheduled reconcile job and notification path.

critical_path.why: Without an isolated project namespace, Switchboard work either pollutes Maxwell/Helm or remains invisible.
The live host must expose project=switchboard before agents can dogfood against it.
Codex is part of the daily development loop; the protocol is not real until Codex stops relying on memory.
Cost-per-outcome is the commercial wedge; spend without KPI/outcome mapping is only telemetry.
A board that cannot prove GitHub truth will drift the moment multiple agents touch the repo.

milestones.name: Switchboard board live
milestones.gate_criteria: project=switchboard appears in the web switcher and MCP; DOGFOOD-1/DOGFOOD-2 evidence is recorded.

milestones.name: First dogfood loop
milestones.gate_criteria: One real Switchboard change is claimed, completed with evidence, merged, and reconciled.

milestones.name: Adapter P0
milestones.gate_criteria: Claude Code and Codex adapters can pass the P0 handshake without relying on a human prompt ritual.

milestones.name: Economics visible
milestones.gate_criteria: At least one outcome is linked to a KPI and a task-level cost tally.

consolidated_risks.risk: Dogfood can become circular theatre if the board records ceremony but does not block or reveal bad behavior.
consolidated_risks.mitigation: Keep the experiment small and evidence-driven: adapters must perform the handshake, completion must carry branch/SHA/PR evidence, and reconcile must surface contradictions.

consolidated_risks.risk: Adding Switchboard to the same planner app could accidentally cross-write Maxwell or Helm.
consolidated_risks.mitigation: Use a separate SQLite file, fail closed on unknown projects, and require explicit project=switchboard for product work.

consolidated_risks.risk: Tally slips behind protocol work and the product loses its cost-per-outcome wedge.
consolidated_risks.mitigation: Keep TALLY-1 and TALLY-2 on the critical path; expose rough economics before polishing dashboards.

consolidated_decisions.question: Should Switchboard be tracked inside Maxwell, Helm, or its own project?
consolidated_decisions.recommended_default: Use its own project=switchboard namespace with a separate SQLite file and seed.

consolidated_decisions.question: Is the dogfood board production infrastructure?
consolidated_decisions.recommended_default: No. Treat it as a live experiment until adapters and reconcile prove the loop.

consolidated_decisions.question: Can agents set tasks Done?
consolidated_decisions.recommended_default: No. Agents complete to In Review with evidence; merge/reconcile authority sets Done.
